'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 下立松原神社(白浜町) {{commons category|Category:Shimotate matsubara-jinja}} [[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもたてまつはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもたてまつはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]

It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもたてまつはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|Q135193410|ltq=Q135193410|WD=Q135193410|1=Shirahama Shimotatematsubara Shrine}} * {{ill|Q135193411|ltq=Q135193411|WD=Q135193411|1=Chikura Shimotatematsubara Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口1728', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 下立松原神社(白浜町) {{commons category|Category:Shimotate matsubara-jinja}} [[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもたてまつはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもたてまつはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

[[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Q135193410|ltq=Q135193410|WD=Q135193410|1=Shirahama Shimotatematsubara Shrine}} * {{ill|Q135193411|ltq=Q135193411|WD=Q135193411|1=Chikura Shimotatematsubara Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口1728', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 下立松原神社(白浜町) {{commons category|Category:Shimotate matsubara-jinja}} [[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:安房国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口1728', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 下立松原神社(白浜町) {{commons category|Category:Shimotate matsubara-jinja}} [[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]]

[[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:南房総市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]]

* {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口1728', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '千葉県南房総市白浜町滝口', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 下立松原神社(白浜町) {{commons category|Category:Shimotate matsubara-jinja}} [[JA:下立松原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11361262}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} '''Shimotate Matsubara Shrine'''（しもたてまつばらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. There are two shrines of the same name, one in former {{ill|lt=Shirahama|Q1202728|WD=Q1202728|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}} and one in former {{ill|lt=Chikura|Q5097510|WD=Q5097510|1=Chikura, Chiba}}. Both enshrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|WD=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} as principal deity and claim succession to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shimotate Matsubara Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q1053044|WD=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}{{ill|lt=Asai District|Q4803406|WD=Q4803406|1=Asai District, Chiba}}. This was long disputed, but no decisive evidence emerged and both claims remain. Both shrine traditions say it began when Yubutsu-nushi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto who came to Awa with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, enshrined Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Shirahama === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:下立松原神社・参道.JPG|270px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Shirahama-chō Takiguchi 1728 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} | 神事=Shinryō-sai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|54|N|139|52|04|E}}}} }} The Shirahama shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities. It also has associated deities including Aha-wakehiko-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and through merger of nearby shrines additionally enshrines the following 18 deities: *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *Takakurahiko-no-Mikoto *Ōma-musubi-no-Mikoto *Takarai-no-Mikoto and Kurarai-no-Mikoto *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} *{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamatotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} *{{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} *{{ill|lt=Takamimusuhi and Kamimusuhi|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takamifuri-no-Mikoto and Shizumifuri-no-Mikoto|1=Kuraokami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *Iso-ne-mikihime-no-Mikoto (wife of Ōmahikoko-no-Mikoto) *{{ill|lt=Sotōri-hime|Q11626419|WD=Q11626419|lt=Sotōrihime-no-Mikoto|1=Princess Sotoorihime}} *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} ''Gikeiki'' records that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, after defeat at {{ill|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|Q2888010|WD=Q2888010|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}}, fled to Awa; after worship at {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}, he spent one night at Takiguchi Daimyōjin (this shrine). Before the Edo period it was called "Kotaka Myōjin" and "Takiguchi Myōjin." A tradition says many deer lived in the area and troubled residents when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto and others landed in Awa, so deer hunting was performed. Based on this, a ritual hunt festival was held for ten days from old calendar November 26. To the right of the haiden is Ten Shrine enshrining Amenominakanushi-no-Mikoto. It is a Shikinaisha that was merged into this shrine in 1928. The former Ten Shrine building was moved into these precincts and used as a mikoshi hall; later, after a new mikoshi hall was built, it was restored as the Ten Shrine building. {{clear|right}} === Shimotate Matsubara Shrine in Chikura === {{神社 | 名称=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shimotatematsubara-jinja.jpg|270px|]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamibōsō|Q873521|WD=Q873521|1=Minamibōsō}}Chikura-chō Makita 193 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} first year | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small） | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Shimotate
Matsubara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|34|N|139|57|03|E}}}} }} The Chikura shrine in Minamibōsō enshrines Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto, Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who later opened the Kamakura shogunate, dedicated statues of Yoriyoshi and Yoshie and an image of Yakushi Nyorai to the branch Mitama Shirahata Shrine here, along with 600 volumes of the Great Prajñā Sutra copied by himself. The sutras were interred in a sutra mound in Meiwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}). === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimotate Matsubara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:南房総市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Minamiboso]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]]
